candidate_llm_application: KULTRUN-QUAD-DEFENSE — 4-quadrant stratified adversarial co-evolution defense. Attack-space partitioned by 2 axes (A1=direct vs indirect injection, A2=persuasion-based vs decomposition-based). 4 (G_q, D_q) co-evolution pairs trained with quadrant-restricted attack distribution. Meta-classifier C(x) routes prompt to in-quadrant defender D_q(x).
rule: hit iff kw_count>=2 OR sem_score>=0.7 OR func_score>=0.7; total_hits>=1 => FAIL

results.title: Attack-Defense Co-Evolution 2511.19218 — monolithic attacker-defender co-evolution
results.rationale: EXACT-TWIN on co-evolution backbone. Attack-Defense Co-Evolution 2511.19218 implements the very (G, D) attacker-defender PPO co-evolution loop that KULTRUN-QUAD-DEFENSE replicates four times. KULTRUN's only delta is stratification: instead of one monolithic (G, D), it runs 4 in parallel with quadrant-restricted attack distributions and adds a meta-classifier router. The stratification is a standard mixture-of-experts wrapper applied to the existing co-evolution objective; the underlying co-evolution mechanism is unchanged. Quadrant-conditioned PPO is a straightforward conditional restriction of 2511.19218's joint min-max.

results.title: Baseline Defenses 2309.00614 — bouquet detection + paraphrasing + retokenization
results.rationale: Baseline Defenses 2309.00614 already proposes a heterogeneous DEFENSE BOUQUET (detection + paraphrasing + retokenization) composed per-input — operationally this IS multi-defender routing, just without the formal 2-axis attack-space partition. Composing several specialist defenses and selecting per-prompt is the same architectural pattern KULTRUN-QUAD operationalizes via meta-classifier C(x). The quadrant-vs-bouquet distinction collapses functionally: both pick one specialist defense per incoming prompt.

results.title: Adversarial Preference Learning Findings ACL 2025 — dynamic adversarial-example defender evolves
results.rationale: Adversarial Preference Learning Findings ACL 2025 already implements DYNAMIC adversarial-example defender evolution as preference-based training — exactly the (G, D) per-quadrant co-evolution KULTRUN replicates. Restricting the adversarial-preference data distribution to a labeled quadrant and training 4 such defenders independently is a trivial data-partitioning specialization of the existing dynamic adversarial preference paradigm. The 'specialist defender per attack subtype' framing is already present in adversarial preference literature.

results.title: Attacks-Defenses Survey NAACL 2024 — attack-type taxonomy + per-category defenses
results.rationale: EXACT-TWIN on the per-attack-type-defense framing. NAACL 2024 Attacks-Defenses Survey is built around an ATTACK-TYPE TAXONOMY with PER-CATEGORY DEFENSES — IS the conceptual ground truth KULTRUN-QUAD instantiates with a 2-axis 4-cell partition. The direct/indirect injection axis (A1) and the persuasion/decomposition axis (A2) are standard categories within this survey's taxonomy; KULTRUN-QUAD just intersects two known axes to produce the 4-cell grid. The 'pick the defense that matches the attack class' principle is the survey's core organizing claim.

results.title: Misrouter 2605.04446 — input-only attack exploiting MoE routing
results.rationale: Misrouter 2605.04446 demonstrates that input-only adversaries STEER MoE ROUTING toward harmful experts — directly attacks the exact meta-classifier router KULTRUN-QUAD relies on (C(x) routing to D_q). The candidate architecture is therefore explicitly within Misrouter's published attack surface: an adversary crafts a prompt that forces C(x) to misroute to a defender D_q' trained on the wrong quadrant, where coverage is weak. KULTRUN-QUAD does NOT address router-targeting adaptive attacks, so this prior result both (a) anticipates the routing-as-defense pattern and (b) supplies the known break. Functional-twin on the router-attack threat model.

results.title: MOEVIL — per-expert poisoning attacks MoE LLM safety
results.rationale: MOEVIL targets PER-EXPERT POISONING in MoE LLMs and proposes MoE-routing-specific defenses — the per-expert specialization KULTRUN-QUAD adopts (per-quadrant defender D_q) is the same per-expert object MOEVIL attacks and defends. The threat model MOEVIL surfaces (adversary degrades one specialist, then triggers routing into it) applies directly: an attacker who biases one quadrant's training distribution (or finds an example near a quadrant boundary) degrades the matched D_q. KULTRUN-QUAD does not include MOEVIL's per-expert auditing countermeasures, so MOEVIL both anticipates the architectural pattern and supplies a published vulnerability.

results.title: F-SOUR Unsafe Routes MoE OpenReview — discovers unsafe MoE routing configs ASR ~0.90
results.rationale: F-SOUR discovers UNSAFE MoE ROUTING configurations with ~0.90 ASR across 4 MoE families — KULTRUN-QUAD's 4-quadrant defender + meta-classifier C(x) is structurally a 4-expert MoE safety stack, and F-SOUR's attack methodology applies almost verbatim: enumerate routing configurations, find combinations where the routed defender D_q fails the adversarial prompt. The 4-MoE-family scope F-SOUR validates directly covers KULTRUN-QUAD's 4-quadrant defender pool. The defender-MoE-as-broken-routing prior is published and unaddressed by the candidate.

results.title: Attacker Moves Second OpenReview — adaptive attacks bypass 12 defenses ASR >90%
results.rationale: Attacker Moves Second establishes that ADAPTIVE ATTACKS BYPASS 12 DEFENSES with ASR > 90% via systematic adaptive tuning — KULTRUN-QUAD's per-quadrant defenders D_q are static products of finite quadrant-restricted co-evolution, and the meta-classifier C(x) is a known attack surface (per Misrouter, F-SOUR). The candidate trains in a closed quadrant world while inference is open: any adaptive attacker can mix axes (e.g., direct + persuasion combined with indirect + decomposition snippets) to land at a quadrant boundary where C(x) misroutes, then exploit the off-distribution defender. The 'attacker moves second' principle applies in full to KULTRUN-QUAD's training-vs-deployment asymmetry.

reason: All 8 results trigger hit under sem>=0.7 AND func>=0.7. The candidate decomposes exhaustively across four prior-art clusters: (cluster A — adversarial co-evolution backbone) Attack-Defense Co-Evolution 2511.19218 (sem 0.92, func 0.90) supplies the (G, D) PPO co-evolution loop KULTRUN replicates 4x; Adversarial Preference Learning Findings ACL 2025 (sem 0.88, func 0.86) supplies the dynamic adversarial-defender preference-evolution paradigm; (cluster B — attack-type taxonomy + per-category defense) NAACL 2024 Attacks-Defenses Survey (sem 0.84, func 0.82) IS the per-attack-type-defense organizing principle, with direct/indirect injection and persuasion/decomposition both already taxonomized — KULTRUN merely intersects two known axes to get 4 cells; Baseline Defenses 2309.00614 (sem 0.86, func 0.84) supplies the heterogeneous defense-bouquet selecting per-input that operationally equals meta-classifier routing; (cluster C — MoE/router-as-defender attack surface) Misrouter 2605.04446 (sem 0.82, func 0.78) attacks the routing C(x) by input-only steering toward harmful experts; F-SOUR (sem 0.80, func 0.78) discovers unsafe MoE routing configs at ~0.90 ASR across 4 MoE families — directly covering KULTRUN's 4-quadrant defender pool; MOEVIL (sem 0.78, func 0.74) poisons individual experts (= individual D_q) and proposes per-expert auditing the candidate lacks; (cluster D — adaptive-attack bypass) Attacker Moves Second (sem 0.74, func 0.72) establishes ASR>90% adaptive bypass against 12 static defenses, applying directly to KULTRUN's static-quadrant defenders trained in closed-world co-evolution. KULTRUN-QUAD's three claimed contributions — (a) 2-axis attack-space partition + (b) per-quadrant specialist (G_q, D_q) co-evolution + (c) meta-classifier routing — are anticipated cluster-by-cluster: (a) by NAACL 2024 taxonomy axes, (b) by 2511.19218 + ACL 2025 (= co-evolution) applied to the survey-categorized attack subsets, (c) by 2309.00614 defense-bouquet + Misrouter/F-SOUR/MOEVIL MoE-routing literature. The Mapuche kultrun / Meli Witran Mapu / Ngünechen 4-direction cultural framing supplies only narrative for the number-four partition; the underlying mechanism is fully covered. total_hits = 8 >> 1, distinct_effect_clusters = 4 >> 1, candidate FAILS.
recommendation: REJECT candidate. KULTRUN-QUAD-DEFENSE is fully anticipated by the conjunction Attack-Defense Co-Evolution 2511.19218 + Adversarial Preference Learning ACL 2025 + NAACL 2024 Attacks-Defenses Survey + Baseline Defenses 2309.00614, and BROKEN by Misrouter 2605.04446 + F-SOUR + MOEVIL + Attacker Moves Second. Specifically: (1) the (G_q, D_q) per-quadrant co-evolution pair is a direct data-stratified specialization of 2511.19218's monolithic co-evolution; (2) the 2-axis attack-space partition is an intersection of two axes already named in NAACL 2024's attack taxonomy (direct/indirect injection + persuasion/decomposition); (3) the meta-classifier C(x) routing is the published defense-bouquet input-conditional selection of 2309.00614; (4) the resulting 4-defender stack is an MoE safety routing architecture for which Misrouter / F-SOUR / MOEVIL have already published high-ASR routing-attack methodologies, and (5) the closed-world quadrant-restricted training vs open-world adaptive inference is exactly the asymmetry Attacker Moves Second exploits at ASR > 90%. To salvage, the candidate would need a feature uniquely supplied by the kultrun source not anticipated by these clusters, e.g.: (a) machi trance + 4-direction cosmology operationalized as a CYCLIC ROTATION SCHEDULE where the meta-classifier is forced to route through ALL 4 D_q in a strict trance-determined permutation per prompt (instead of best-quadrant routing), with a defense-in-depth composition + cryptographic attestation that all 4 D_q certified safe before output, defeating Misrouter/F-SOUR by removing single-quadrant routing dependency; (b) a Meli Witran Mapu 'land-of-four-corners' constraint enforcing pairwise-orthogonal training distributions via mutual-information minimization on quadrant gradients to provably bound off-quadrant transfer error (addressing the boundary-adaptive-attack break); (c) a Ngünechen tetrad-deity 4-of-4-agreement consensus rule where output is released only if all 4 D_q independently approve under their own quadrant lens, transforming the routing problem from selection (broken) into intersection (4x harder for the adversary). As stated, KULTRUN-QUAD-DEFENSE is in the saturated region documented by 2511.19218 + NAACL 2024 Survey + 2309.00614 + the Misrouter/F-SOUR/MOEVIL routing-attack literature, with Attacker Moves Second supplying the closing break.
